title: ImageJ plate count protocol
steps: Capture Image Using a high-resolution camera set on 'Close-up' mode (the setting with the flower a ) take a clear picture of the plate. Name and save the image.
Open ImageJ a ImageJ MAC OS, Linux, Windows
Open Image click on File -> Open -> and open the image
Change image to 8 bit select Image -> Type -> 8 bit a

Adjust Threshold 1. select Image -> Adjust -> Threshold... a 2. Adjust threshold settings so that just the colonies are highlighted in red. a a 3. Click Apply. This will turn the image into a black and white binary image.

Apply Watershed select Process -> Binary -> Watershed This will seperate out colonies ahat are touching so they will be counted seperately. a The image must be saved in binary form to apply watershed.

Count colonies Using the ImageJ tools draw a circle around the coloneis to be counted. a select Analyze -> Analyze Particles a I have not been able to manually seperate out colonies that the watershed does not recognize so the final count is often underestimated.
Colony count data Summary tab gives total count a
creator: Jeric Harper
